<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Draft render smoke test validates streaming frame order and stability</title>
  <subtitle>Test suite checks body→thinking→tools order and prefix stability to prevent UI animation glitches during streaming</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>check-draft-render.mjs validates renderDraftReply() output ordering: body first, thinking second, tools last</fact>
    <fact>Test ensures tool additions don't change body prefix to prevent Telegram client animation regression</fact>
    <fact>Telegram client animates draft updates by showing strlen(prev) chars immediately, then fading in new chars</fact>
    <fact>Test written as regression guard for 2026-07-10 draft streaming display issue where tool insertions caused text to appear to retreat</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The check-draft-render.mjs smoke test validates critical streaming UI behavior for Telegram draft messages. Telegram's client-side animation treats draft updates as diff-based: it immediately displays the first strlen(previous) characters and fades in the remaining new content. If renderReply() places tools at the head and a new tool line completes, the head insertion causes the client to hide body text at the tail (user sees displayed text retreat). The test verifies renderDraftReply() uses body→thinking→tools ordering to maintain append-only semantics, and that adding tool lines preserves the complete body prefix unchanged. This prevents animation glitches during streaming. The test operates on compiled dist/telegram-ui.js output and includes fixtures for various buffer/thinking/tool combinations.</narrative>
